attached_docs: KFM GitHub Repository Documentation Implementation Agent — Full Operating Prompt v3.1
Kansas Frontier Matrix — AI Build Operating Contract.md

validation_gates.reason: Repository, pinned base, target path, two-file change budget, API-only review-branch route, non-goals, acceptance criteria, validation plan, and stop conditions were resolved before mutation.

Repository identity, ID, visibility, permissions, default branch, immutable base SHA, target blob, path-scoped instruction checks, pull-request/branch concurrency, PR template, CODEOWNERS, and workflow triggers were inspected.

The README remains under the canonical tests responsibility root and existing Agriculture domain segment. Directory Rules, proposed ADR-0001, and the drift register were inspected; no new root or parallel schema, contract, policy, source, registry, evidence, release, or proof home is created.

No duplicate document ID, open pull request, or task branch surfaced in bounded connector searches. Version v0.2 supersedes the short prior README at the same path and preserves parent/child authority boundaries.

The parent, eight child READMEs, one docstring-only placeholder Python file, fixture/contract/schema/policy/validator indexes, Makefile, workflows, and generated-receipt schema were inspected. Unknown unindexed or differently named files remain explicitly bounded.

The revision covers test scope and non-scope, fixture families and routing, valid/invalid/denied/abstained/held/restricted/quarantined/error/correction/rollback cases, no-network and live-test separation, repository-grounded commands, finite outcomes, validation layers, rights/sensitivity, failure interpretation, limits of passing tests, CI boundaries, maintenance, and fixture updates.

The prior purpose, status, evidence basis, authority split, eight child lanes, test responsibilities, allowed/disallowed content, no-network posture, validation guidance, and open questions were retained and expanded.

The Agriculture, docs-build, link-check, and citation-validation workflows trigger on pull requests but perform checkout plus TODO echo commands only. No inspected secret, privileged write, release, or deployment behavior is introduced or relied on.

The UTF-8 README has one H1, a final newline, balanced fenced blocks, unique heading slugs, 51 internal links with no unresolved target under the local GitHub-style slug check, and no duplicate explicit IDs.

No credentials, private endpoints, living-person identities, real field/operator/parcel records, proprietary measures, restricted source payloads, or exact sensitive locations were introduced. The README requires synthetic public-safe fixtures and fail-closed exact exposure.
No common private-key, GitHub token, AWS access-key, or OpenAI key pattern was found in the authored README or receipt.
The README SHA-256 is 1b3601766c8cfd0e0fc676f8defaed55bba60cea150e723f1c120ab3fdbf3ef8; the generated receipt names the exact artifact path and digest.
The scoped change creates no executable test, fixture, schema, policy, validator, workflow, runtime behavior, or release behavior; current Agriculture executable coverage is not established.
Remote pull-request checks are not available until branch and PR creation, and the inspected relevant workflows are TODO-only scaffolds rather than substantive validation.

The receipt was checked against the inspected generated-receipt contract requirements: required fields, semantic-version and hash formats, allowed truth labels and gate outcomes, citation records, human-review state, and optional link structure.

notes: AI-authored documentation-only revision prepared against pinned main-branch evidence under a two-file change budget. The target remains an enforceability README, not an executable test or authority surface. Human review remains pending; this receipt records provenance and validation, not merge, promotion, source activation, policy approval, scientific fitness, release, rollback execution, or publication.
